minutes_briefs.title: BC liquor licence renewal fees change for 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has amended the Liquor Control and Licensing Regulation to change the fee structure for renewing certain liquor licences and endorsements. The new rules set different fees based on how much liquor a business buys or sells, with new dollar brackets and rates. For example, a licence that expires before May 1, 2026, can still use the old fee schedule. The changes affect liquor primary, food primary, catering, licensee retail stores, wine stores, special wine stores, rural licensee retail stores, and distilleries with on-site store endorsements.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check your licence renewal date and calculate your liquor purchases or sales to determine which fee bracket applies.

key_dates.label: New liquor licence fee rules take effect — BC

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 43/2026
Made under: Liquor Control And Licensing Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: March 25, 2026

B.C. Reg. 43/2026, deposited March 25, 2026, under theLIQUOR CONTROL AND LICENSING ACT[section 83]. Order in Council 105/2026, approved and ordered March 25, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Administrator, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that (a)	the Liquor Control and Licensing Regulation, B.C. Reg. 241/2016, is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 1, and (b)	effective May 1, 2026, the Liquor Control and Licensing Regulation is amended as set out in the attached Appendix 2. — L. POPHAM,Minister of Agriculture and Food; D. GIBSON,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Section 202 of the Liquor Control and Licensing Regulation, B.C. Reg. 241/2016, is amended by adding the following subsection: (3) Despite items 19 to 31 of Schedule 1, if an application is made to renew a licence or endorsement described in any of those items that expires before May 1, 2026, the fee that must be submitted under this section is the applicable fee under Schedule 1 as it read immediately before the coming into force of this subsection. 2	Schedule 1 is amended by striking out items 19 to 31 and substituting the following: Liquor primary, food primary, catering, licensee retail store, wine store, special wine store or rural licensee retail store: if the amount of liquor purchases or sales calculated under section 204 of this regulation is (b) over $12 500 and up to $20 000 (c) over $20 000 and up to $45 000 (d) over $45 000 and up to $100 000 (e) over $100 000 and up to $500 000 (f) over $500 000 and up to $1 000 000 (g) over $1 000 000 and up to $2 000 000 Distillery with manufacturer on-site store endorsement, special event area endorsement or endorsement or special event area 0.13 per hectolitre for each hectolitre produced over 9 167 hectolitres, and up
